crime.value: The disappearance/suspected murder of Edwin Drood

victim.value: Edwin Drood - young man whose disappearance forms the central mystery

closed_world.value: The cathedral town of Cloisterham with its close-knit community centered around the Cathedral Close
closed_world.quote: the quiet Close with specks of light... all Cloisterham would have stood aghast

investigator.value: Third-person omniscient narrator following multiple characters, particularly focusing on the suspicious John Jasper
investigator.quote: Why does he move so softly to-night? No outward reason is apparent for it.

suspect_architecture.value: Early establishment of Jasper as central suspicious figure with secretive nocturnal behavior, supported by secondary figures including Durdles and the opium den inhabitants
suspect_architecture.quote: Why does he move so softly to-night? No outward reason is apparent for it. Can there be any sympathetic reason crouching darkly within him?

red_herring.value: The opium-induced Oriental fantasy sequence creates deliberate confusion about reality versus hallucination, misdirecting from the actual mystery mechanics
red_herring.quote: An ancient English Cathedral Tower? How can the ancient English Cathedral tower be here!

clue_fairness.value: Jasper's deliberate soft movement and secretive behavior with bottle and change of clothes presented directly to reader without interpretation
clue_fairness.quote: softly changes his coat for a pea-jacket, with a goodly wicker-cased bottle in its largest pocket

social_world.value: Cathedral hierarchy with Dean, choir master Jasper, and church officials like Tope establishing institutional power structures in Cloisterham
social_world.quote: the Dean withdraws to his dinner, Mr. Tope to his tea, and Mr. Jasper to his piano

_meta.title: ebook charles dickens the mystery of edwin drood
